title: <Project> tile style reference
notes: Light upper-left, selective dark outline, soft drop shadow, 3/4 top-down. <fill in the rules a palette can't encode>.
_comment_palette: 'auto' quantizes the exemplars below into a swatch row, OR give a path to a .gpl / .hex palette file (e.g. 'references/palette.gpl') to use the locked ramps verbatim. Paths resolve relative to THIS manifest file.

exemplars._comment: 1) A single image. Order exemplars simple -> detailed; the simplest sets the detail floor, the most detailed the ceiling.
exemplars.label: oak (detailed hero)

exemplars._comment: 2) A SHEET: one PNG that is a grid of frames. 'frame' is one cell's [w,h]; 'grid' is [cols,rows]. The whole grid is sliced and laid out as one animation row.
exemplars.label: idle (8-frame sheet)

exemplars._comment: 2b) Same sheet, but only specific cells (0-based, row-major) — use to thin a long strip down to readable keyframes.
exemplars.label: idle (frames 0,3,6)

exemplars._comment: 3) A frame STRIP: a list of already-sliced NN.png frames, laid out as one animation row so the motion reads left-to-right.
exemplars.label: idle frames
